metadata.title: City Tax Knowledge Base - Consolidated Context
metadata.purpose: Context package for an internal agent answering questions about tourist/city taxes by region/city.
metadata.scope_note: This package consolidates information shared in the working discussion and uploaded internal datasets. It is not a full authoritative legal database and should be treated as a governed operational knowledge base.
recommended_usage: Use this as context for internal Q&A and project planning.
Do not present every city entry as legally authoritative unless separately validated against official sources.
Where confidence is high but classification is missing, position results as structured operational intelligence rather than certified legal truth.

coverage_concentration.narrative: A phased rollout focused on the top 50-100 cities captures most of the high-density operational footprint and likely ~75-85% of high-volume European urban tourism.

strategic_conclusions.key_takeaway: The current tax calculator can be naturally amplified into a structured European Tourist Tax Intelligence layer.
why: The dataset is large enough to be meaningful.
Rule-type agreement is extremely high across cities.
Most major tourism cities are structurally stable.
The main gap is the lack of explicit accommodation classification (hotel stars / official category).

impact: Some cities (e.g. Barcelona, France) have tax tiers that vary by hotel class or accommodation type.
Observed host-configured values do not automatically equal the official legal tier structure.
A city-level regulatory model is stronger than a purely host-derived average.

delivery_models.description: Structured dataset for internal quote simulations + authoritative validation API at booking confirmation.

commercial_working_assumptions.go_live_rule: Recurring fee starts when the solution goes live in production.

high_confidence_countries: Spain (core regions such as Catalonia and Balearics)
Czech Republic (structurally consistent, but pricing variance in Prague requires refinement)

official_logic_shared_in_workstream.rule_shape: fixed per person per night, tiered by accommodation category, with Barcelona municipal surcharge

city_profiles.confidence: high for structure, medium for exact tier mapping unless category is explicitly stored

global_complexity_notes.europe: Mostly municipality-based, often fixed per person per night, with caps, exemptions and in some cases category-based tiers.
global_complexity_notes.france: Higher complexity due to municipal + departmental + regional layers and classification-based logic.

united_states.summary: A stacked percentage-based model rather than a single city tax.
common_layers: special district or tourism improvement fees

known_limitations: The analyzed internal dataset does not explicitly include hotel star rating or official accommodation category.
Some internal city configurations may exist even where a formal municipal tax does not currently exist in law.
For category-driven cities, exact legal calculation requires either explicit category data or an official tier table.
recommended_next_steps: Maintain a structured CityTaxProfile table rather than only host-derived averages.
For top 50-100 European cities, build or validate tier tables manually against official sources.
For internal Q&A, always label answers by confidence level.
